brief_justification: Arrives at exactly $19,160 with valid elasticity arithmetic; goes one level beyond surface by explaining why X revenue fell despite the price rise and noting the net $160 gain, but no second/third-order effects explored; makes a pre-computation prediction and explicitly flags the divergence from expectation, showing process awareness; verifies initial-state totals and compares prediction to result but no boundary-condition or alternative-angle check; presents all conclusions as certain with no acknowledgment of linearity assumptions or model limitations; only one computational approach considered, no alternatives examined; every arithmetic step is fully shown and reproducible end-to-end; the negative gate concerns marketing attribution which is irrelevant to this math problem so the agent avoids it by default without explicitly naming why; the agent's reformulated injection suppresses overconfidence and model lock, which it partially addresses via the divergence check, but exhibits slight single-hypothesis commitment; the topology specifies factual/counterfactual forks and SUTVA gates that are absent — the agent proceeds entirely linearly through the calculation.
